_doc: Snapshot of which (theme, check-title) pairs are already failing on
origin/main. When FIFTY_ALLOW_BASELINE_FAILURES=1 (which pre-commit,
pre-push, and CI's theme-gate set automatically), bin/check.py prints
these as WARN-BASELINE in yellow and does NOT count them toward its
exit code -- only NEW failures, introduced by the current branch,
can block a commit/push/PR.
Why this file exists
The project treats bin/check.py as a hard gate (pre-commit, pre-push,
CI theme-gate), but main has always carried a small amount of latent
debt (e.g. Foundry's accent-on-contrast hover state is 2.14:1 vs the
3:1 floor). That debt was irrelevant to every PR that wasn't trying
to fix it, but it blocked every PR anyway, forcing agents to reach
for `git commit --no-verify` on unrelated work. This file flips the
default: pre-existing debt is tracked explicitly here and waved
through on feature branches; CI on main is still the source of
truth for what debt exists. New regressions still block.
How to regenerate
`python3 bin/check.py --save-baseline-failures` regenerates this
file from the CURRENT tree. Run it while checked out on origin/main
(or let pre-commit/pre-push auto-refresh via a detached
`git worktree` against origin/main when it notices staleness).
Every entry must be ONE pair of (theme, check-title). 'theme' is the
top-level folder name that contains the failing theme.json; 'check'
is the exact string passed to Result(...) for the failing check (so
renaming a check correctly invalidates the baseline entry and the
gate re-tightens). Checks that are not theme-scoped (i.e. that only
fire from the repo root) record 'theme: "_repo"' .
